_comment: Reference list of MCP servers this stack was built around. NAMES and WHAT EACH NEEDS only — every credential below is a placeholder. Copy the entries you actually use into your settings.json mcpServers block (or claude mcp add) and fill in real values in your own untracked env/credentials store, never in a committed file.

x._what: X/Twitter read/write bridge. Local HTTP MCP server you run yourself (e.g. a Playwright/cookie-session bridge) or a hosted X MCP server.
_needs: a running local bridge process bound to this port, or swap in a hosted server URL

codex._what: OpenAI Codex CLI as an MCP server, for second-opinion code review / cross-model checks.
_needs: npm i -g @openai/codex (or your distro's package) so codex-mcp-server resolves via PATH

whatsapp._what: WhatsApp Web bridge (e.g. a Baileys-based Node server) exposing send/list/search tools over MCP.
_needs: your own WhatsApp Web MCP server implementation (not included — Baileys auth sessions are credentials, never ship them)
a one-time QR-code login that persists an auth/ session dir locally; gitignore it

hermes._what: Multi-platform messaging bridge (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix) under one MCP surface.
_needs: per-platform bot tokens / session credentials, set as env vars, never committed

nimble._what: Nimble web-scraping/proxy API for fetching pages that block plain requests.

gws._what: Google Workspace bridge — Gmail, Calendar, Drive — over MCP.
_needs: a Google Cloud OAuth client (Desktop app type)
one-time browser OAuth consent flow, token cached locally under your own $GOOGLE_TOKEN_DIR

slack._what: Slack workspace bridge — read channels/threads, post messages, manage reactions.
_needs: a Slack app + bot token (xoxb-...) with the scopes your use case needs

github._what: GitHub bridge — issues, PRs, repos, releases, code search.
_needs: a GitHub personal access token (fine-grained, scoped to the repos you need)

conversation-search._what: Query layer over the recall-db schema (see infra-templates/recall-db) — search past Claude Code conversations.
_needs: run infra-templates/recall-db/setup.py first to create the empty db
an indexer (not included) to populate it from your transcripts
your own thin MCP server exposing search/list over the schema

knowledge-base._what: Query layer over the knowledge-base schema (see infra-templates/knowledge-base) — get_topic / add_insight / list_topics / search_knowledge.
_needs: run infra-templates/knowledge-base/setup.py first to create the empty db
your own thin MCP server exposing the four operations over the schema
